packages.name: pdfkit
packages.synopsis: Package pdfkit is a small, dependency-free library for generating PDF documents in pure Go.
packages.doc:
Package pdfkit is a small, dependency-free library for generating PDF
documents in pure Go. It uses only the Go standard library: no cgo and no
third-party modules.

# Overview

A [Document] is a container of [Page] values plus document metadata. You
build a document by adding pages, drawing text, vector graphics and images
onto each page, and then serializing the result:

	doc := pdfkit.New()
	doc.SetTitle("Hello")
	page := doc.AddPage(pdfkit.A4)
	page.SetFont(pdfkit.Helvetica, 24)
	page.DrawText(72, 720, "Hello, PDF!")
	data, _ := doc.Bytes() // or doc.Write(w) / doc.Save("out.pdf")

# The PDF object model

The generated file is a conforming PDF 1.7 document. Internally pdfkit emits
the standard indirect-object structure:

  - a %PDF-1.7 header and a binary marker comment;
  - a document catalog (/Type /Catalog) referencing the page tree;
  - a page tree (/Type /Pages) with one page node per [Page];
  - one content stream per page holding the drawing operators;
  - font, image (XObject) and /Info dictionaries as needed;
  - a cross-reference (xref) table, a trailer and %%EOF.

Output is deterministic: given the same drawing calls, the byte stream is
identical, which makes golden-file testing straightforward. The [Reader] type
provides a minimal xref parser that can read the structure back.

# Coordinate system

Pages use native PDF user space. The origin (0, 0) is the bottom-left corner,
x increases rightward and y increases upward, with distances in points
(1/72 inch). Standard page sizes ([A4], [Letter], [Legal] and others) are
provided, along with [Custom] sizes and [PageSize.Landscape] orientation.

# Fonts and text

All 14 standard PDF Type1 fonts are available as package variables
([Helvetica], [TimesRoman], [Courier], [Symbol], [ZapfDingbats] and their
bold/italic variants) and by name via [StandardFont]. Each font ships with a
built-in Adobe Font Metrics width table, so [Font.Width] and [Page.TextWidth]
return accurate text widths for layout. [Page.DrawText] draws a single line,
[Page.DrawLines] draws several, and [Page.DrawParagraph] performs greedy word
wrapping to a maximum width. Text color follows the current fill color set
with [Page.SetFillColor].

# Vector graphics

Paths are built with [Page.MoveTo], [Page.LineTo], [Page.CurveTo] (cubic
Bézier), [Page.Rect], [Page.Circle] and [Page.Ellipse], then painted with
[Page.Stroke], [Page.Fill] or [Page.FillStroke]. Line width is set with
[Page.SetLineWidth] and colors with [Page.SetStrokeColor] and
[Page.SetFillColor]. Colors are RGB values constructed with [RGB] or [Gray].

# Images

PNG and JPEG images are embedded as image XObjects. JPEG data is stored
directly using the DCTDecode filter; PNG pixels are re-encoded as
zlib-compressed RGB samples (FlateDecode) with an automatically generated soft
mask when the source has transparency. Load images with [LoadImage],
[LoadPNG] or [LoadJPEG] and place them with [Page.DrawImage].

vars.doc:
The 14 standard fonts. These are always available in every conforming PDF
viewer and never need to be embedded.

Standard page sizes in points (1 point = 1/72 inch). Sizes are given in
portrait orientation; use Landscape to rotate.

types.name: BlendMode
types.signature: type BlendMode string
types.doc:
BlendMode names a PDF separable or non-separable blend mode used when
compositing transparent content.

consts.signature:
const (
	BlendNormal     BlendMode = "Normal"
	BlendMultiply   BlendMode = "Multiply"
	BlendScreen     BlendMode = "Screen"
	BlendOverlay    BlendMode = "Overlay"
	BlendDarken     BlendMode = "Darken"
	BlendLighten    BlendMode = "Lighten"
	BlendColorDodge BlendMode = "ColorDodge"
	BlendColorBurn  BlendMode = "ColorBurn"
	BlendHardLight  BlendMode = "HardLight"
	BlendSoftLight  BlendMode = "SoftLight"
	BlendDifference BlendMode = "Difference"
	BlendExclusion  BlendMode = "Exclusion"
	BlendHue        BlendMode = "Hue"
	BlendSaturation BlendMode = "Saturation"
	BlendColor      BlendMode = "Color"
	BlendLuminosity BlendMode = "Luminosity"
)

types.name: Bookmark
types.doc: Bookmark is a handle to a single outline entry, used to attach child entries.

methods.name: Add
methods.signature: func (bm *Bookmark) Add(title string, page *Page, y float64) *Bookmark
methods.doc: Add appends a child bookmark beneath this one.

types.name: CMYKColor
types.doc:
CMYKColor is a color in the subtractive CMYK (cyan, magenta, yellow, key/black)
color space, with each component in the range [0, 1]. CMYK is the native color
model of most print workflows; PDF supports it directly through the
DeviceCMYK color space and the "k"/"K" content operators. Construct one with
CMYK and paint with Page.SetFillCMYK or Page.SetStrokeCMYK.

funcs.name: CMYK
funcs.signature: func CMYK(c, m, y, k float64) CMYKColor
funcs.doc: CMYK constructs a CMYKColor from four components, each clamped to [0, 1].

methods.name: RGB
methods.doc:
RGB converts the CMYK color to its RGB equivalent using the standard naive
conversion R=(1-C)(1-K), G=(1-M)(1-K), B=(1-Y)(1-K). The result is suitable
for on-screen preview; exact print reproduction depends on the output device's
color profile.

types.name: Color
types.doc: Color is an RGB color with components in the range [0, 1].

funcs.name: Gray
funcs.doc: Gray constructs a grayscale Color from an 8-bit level (0=black, 255=white).

funcs.name: HSL
funcs.signature: func HSL(h, s, l float64) Color
funcs.doc:
HSL constructs an RGB Color from hue (in degrees, wrapped to [0, 360)),
saturation and lightness (each in [0, 1]). HSL(0,1,0.5) is pure red,
HSL(120,1,0.5) is green and HSL(240,1,0.5) is blue.

funcs.name: Hex
funcs.doc:
Hex parses a CSS-style hex color string such as "#ff8800", "ff8800", "#f80"
or "f80" into a Color. A leading '#' is optional; both 3-digit shorthand and
6-digit forms are accepted. It returns an error for any other length or for
non-hexadecimal digits.

funcs.name: NamedColor
funcs.doc:
NamedColor returns the RGB Color for one of the CSS color keywords PDFKit
ships (for example "aliceblue", "black", "cornflowerblue" or "yellowgreen").
The lookup is case-sensitive and expects the lowercase spelling used by CSS
and PDFKit. The second result reports whether name was recognised.

funcs.name: RGB
funcs.signature: func RGB(r, g, b uint8) Color
funcs.doc: RGB constructs a Color from 8-bit components (0..255).

methods.name: CMYK
methods.doc:
CMYK converts an RGB color to CMYK. The black component K is derived as
1-max(R,G,B) and the chromatic components are computed relative to it. Pure
black (0,0,0) maps to K=1 with zero chroma.

methods.name: Hex
methods.doc:
Hex formats the color as a lowercase 6-digit "#rrggbb" hex string. Each
component is rounded to the nearest 8-bit value.

types.name: Document
types.doc:
Document is a PDF document under construction. Create one with New, add pages
with AddPage, draw on each returned Page, then serialize with Write, Bytes or
Save.

The zero value is not usable; always start from New.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc:
New creates an empty document with default metadata (a Producer/Creator of
"pdfkit").

methods.name: AddNamedDest
methods.signature: func (d *Document) AddNamedDest(name string, page *Page, y float64)
methods.doc:
AddNamedDest registers a named destination that points at (implicitly the top
of) page, scrolled so y is near the top. Named destinations can be targeted by
Page.AddLinkToDest and are exposed in the document's /Names /Dests tree.

methods.name: AddPage
methods.doc:
AddPage appends a new page of the given size (portrait orientation as given)
and returns it for drawing.

methods.name: Bytes
methods.doc: Bytes serializes the document to a byte slice of valid PDF-1.7 content.

methods.name: EnableXMP
methods.doc:
EnableXMP generates and attaches an XMP metadata packet built from the current
/Info metadata (Title, Author, Subject, Keywords, Creator, Producer). Call it
after setting metadata and before writing.

methods.name: Outline
methods.doc: Outline returns the document outline, creating it on first use.

methods.name: PageCount
methods.doc: PageCount returns the number of pages added so far.

methods.name: Save
methods.doc: Save writes the document to the named file, creating or truncating it.

methods.name: SetAuthor
methods.doc: SetAuthor sets the document author (/Author).

methods.name: SetCreator
methods.doc: SetCreator sets the creating application name (/Creator).

methods.name: SetEncryption
methods.doc:
SetEncryption enables standard security on the document using the given
options. It must be called before Write/Bytes/Save. RC4-128 is used by
default; set EncryptOptions.UseAES for AES-128.

methods.name: SetKeywords
methods.doc: SetKeywords sets the document keywords (/Keywords).

methods.name: SetProducer
methods.doc: SetProducer sets the producing application name (/Producer).

methods.name: SetSubject
methods.doc: SetSubject sets the document subject (/Subject).

methods.name: SetTitle
methods.doc: SetTitle sets the document title (/Title in the Info dictionary).

methods.name: SetXMP
methods.doc: SetXMP attaches a custom XMP metadata packet verbatim, overriding EnableXMP.

methods.name: Write
methods.doc:
Write serializes the document as PDF-1.7 to w. The output begins with the
"%PDF-1.7" header and ends with "%%EOF", and includes a cross-reference table
and trailer describing every indirect object. Advanced features (annotations,
outlines, form fields, shadings, patterns, transparency, metadata and
encryption) are emitted only when used.

types.name: EncryptOptions
types.doc:
EncryptOptions configures standard PDF document encryption. An empty
UserPassword produces a document that opens without a password but still has
its permission flags and (optionally) an owner password enforced.

types.name: ExtGState
types.doc:
ExtGState is a graphics-state parameter dictionary controlling constant alpha
(transparency) and blend mode. Build one with NewExtGState and apply it to a
page with Page.ApplyExtGState, or use the Page.SetAlpha and Page.SetBlendMode
convenience methods.

funcs.name: NewExtGState
funcs.signature: func NewExtGState(fillAlpha, strokeAlpha float64, blend BlendMode) *ExtGState
funcs.doc:
NewExtGState builds a graphics-state dictionary. fillAlpha and strokeAlpha are
constant alpha values in [0, 1]; pass a negative value to leave one unset. An
empty blend mode is treated as unset.

types.name: Font
types.doc:
Font represents one of the 14 standard PDF Type1 fonts. Each font carries a
built-in glyph-width table (in 1000-unit glyph space) so that text widths can
be measured without embedding or parsing external font files.

Fonts are immutable and safe for concurrent use. Obtain them through the
package-level font variables (for example Helvetica or TimesBold) or via
StandardFont.

funcs.name: LoadTrueType
funcs.doc:
LoadTrueType parses a TrueType or glyf-based OpenType font from r and returns
a Font that will be embedded (with only the glyphs actually used) when the
document is written. Unicode text drawn with this font is encoded via the
font's cmap and mapped back for extraction with a ToUnicode CMap.

funcs.name: LoadTrueTypeFile
funcs.doc:
LoadTrueTypeFile is a convenience wrapper around LoadTrueType that reads from a
file path.

funcs.name: StandardFont
funcs.doc:
StandardFont returns the standard font with the given PDF base-font name
(for example "Helvetica-Bold" or "Times-Roman"). It returns nil if name does
not identify one of the 14 standard fonts.

methods.name: Ascent
methods.doc:
Ascent returns the font ascender — the maximum extent above the baseline — in
points at the given size. This is the recommended distance from the baseline
to the top of the tallest glyphs.

methods.name: CapHeight
methods.doc:
CapHeight returns the cap height (the height of a flat capital letter such as
'H' above the baseline) in points at the given size.

methods.name: Descent
methods.doc:
Descent returns the font descender — the maximum extent below the baseline —
in points at the given size. The value is negative for fonts whose glyphs
descend below the baseline.

methods.name: Embedded
methods.doc:
Embedded reports whether this font is an embedded, subset TrueType/OpenType
font (as opposed to one of the 14 standard fonts).

methods.name: HeightOfString
methods.signature: func (f *Font) HeightOfString(text string, size, maxWidth, lineGap float64) float64
methods.doc:
HeightOfString returns the total height in points needed to render text set in
font f at the given size, wrapped to maxWidth, with lineGap points of extra
leading added between (and after) each line. It measures the number of wrapped
lines and multiplies by the per-line advance, mirroring the accounting used
when laying paragraphs out with Page.DrawTextBox.

methods.name: LineHeight
methods.doc:
LineHeight returns the natural single-line height in points at the given size:
the ascent minus the (negative) descent. Add extra leading between lines as
desired; DrawParagraph and DrawTextBox take an explicit leading value.

methods.name: Width
methods.signature: func (f *Font) Width(text string, size float64) float64
methods.doc:
Width returns the rendered width, in points, of text set in this font at the
given size (in points). It sums the built-in glyph advances; characters
outside the built-in table fall back to the font's default width.

methods.name: XHeight
methods.doc:
XHeight returns the x-height (the height of the lowercase 'x' above the
baseline) in points at the given size. It is zero for fonts that do not report
an x-height, including embedded fonts, Symbol and ZapfDingbats.

types.name: FormField
types.doc:
FormField is an interactive AcroForm field (a text field, checkbox or push
button) placed on a page as a widget annotation. Create fields with
Page.AddTextField, Page.AddCheckbox or Page.AddPushButton.

types.name: GradientStop
types.doc:
GradientStop is one color stop of a gradient: a color positioned at an offset
in [0, 1] along the gradient axis.

types.name: Image
types.doc:
Image is a decoded raster image ready to be embedded in a PDF document as an
XObject. Load one with LoadImage, LoadPNG or LoadJPEG, then draw it onto a
page with Page.DrawImage.

funcs.name: LoadImage
funcs.doc: LoadImage reads an image from r, auto-detecting PNG or JPEG encoding.

funcs.name: LoadJPEG
funcs.doc:
LoadJPEG loads a JPEG image. The original JPEG bytes are embedded directly
using the PDF DCTDecode filter, so no re-encoding occurs.

funcs.name: LoadPNG
funcs.doc:
LoadPNG loads a PNG image. The pixels are decoded and re-encoded as raw,
zlib-compressed RGB samples (PDF FlateDecode). If the source has an alpha
channel, a soft mask (SMask) is generated so transparency is preserved.

types.name: LineCap
types.signature: type LineCap int
types.doc:
LineCap selects the shape drawn at the open ends of a stroked path (the PDF
line cap style, set with the "J" operator).

consts.doc: The three PDF line cap styles.

types.name: LineJoin
types.signature: type LineJoin int
types.doc:
LineJoin selects the shape drawn where two path segments meet (the PDF line
join style, set with the "j" operator).

consts.doc: The three PDF line join styles.

types.name: Outline
types.doc:
Outline is a document outline (bookmark tree). Obtain the document's outline
with Document.Outline, then add top-level bookmarks with Add; each returned
Bookmark can nest further bookmarks beneath it.

methods.name: Add
methods.signature: func (o *Outline) Add(title string, page *Page, y float64) *Bookmark
methods.doc:
Add appends a top-level bookmark titled title that jumps to page at vertical
position y, and returns a handle for nesting children beneath it.

types.name: Page
types.doc:
Page is a single page of a Document and the surface onto which text, vector
graphics and images are drawn.

The coordinate system is native PDF user space: the origin (0, 0) is at the
bottom-left corner of the page, x increases to the right and y increases
upward. Distances are in points (1/72 inch). This matches the underlying PDF
content-stream semantics exactly.

methods.name: AddCheckbox
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddCheckbox(name string, x, y, size float64, checked bool) *FormField
methods.doc:
AddCheckbox adds a checkbox named name at (x, y) with the given side length,
initially checked or not. It returns the field.

methods.name: AddLinkTo
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddLinkTo(x, y, w, h float64, target *Page, atY float64)
methods.doc:
AddLinkTo adds an internal hyperlink over the rectangle at (x, y) that jumps
to target page, scrolled so that atY is near the top of the view.

methods.name: AddLinkToDest
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddLinkToDest(x, y, w, h float64, name string)
methods.doc:
AddLinkToDest adds an internal hyperlink over the rectangle at (x, y) that
jumps to the named destination (see Document.AddNamedDest).

methods.name: AddLinkURI
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddLinkURI(x, y, w, h float64, uri string)
methods.doc:
AddLinkURI adds a hyperlink over the rectangle at (x, y) with width w and
height h that opens the given URI when clicked.

methods.name: AddPushButton
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddPushButton(name string, x, y, w, h float64, caption string) *FormField
methods.doc:
AddPushButton adds a push button named name over the rectangle at (x, y) with
the given caption. It returns the field.

methods.name: AddTextAnnotation
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddTextAnnotation(x, y float64, contents string)
methods.doc:
AddTextAnnotation adds a "sticky note" text annotation with its icon at
(x, y) showing the given contents when opened.

methods.name: AddTextField
methods.signature: func (p *Page) AddTextField(name string, x, y, w, h float64, value string) *FormField
methods.doc:
AddTextField adds a text input field named name over the rectangle at (x, y)
with width w and height h, pre-filled with value. It returns the field.

methods.name: ApplyExtGState
methods.doc:
ApplyExtGState registers g with the document and applies it to the current
graphics state (the "gs" operator). Wrap it in Save/Restore to scope its
effect.

methods.name: Circle
methods.doc:
Circle adds a circle subpath centered at (cx, cy) with the given radius,
approximated by four Bézier curves.

methods.name: Clip
methods.doc:
Clip intersects the current clipping path with the current path using the
nonzero winding rule, then ends the path without painting it (the "W n"
sequence). Construct a path first, call Clip, then draw the content to be
clipped, typically inside a Save/Restore pair.

methods.name: ClipEvenOdd
methods.doc: ClipEvenOdd is like Clip but uses the even-odd rule ("W* n").

methods.name: ClosePath
methods.doc: ClosePath closes the current subpath back to its start point ("h").

methods.name: CurveTo
methods.signature: func (p *Page) CurveTo(x1, y1, x2, y2, x3, y3 float64)
methods.doc:
CurveTo appends a cubic Bézier curve from the current point to (x3, y3) using
(x1, y1) and (x2, y2) as control points.

methods.name: DrawImage
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawImage(img *Image, x, y, width, height float64)
methods.doc:
DrawImage draws img so that it fills the rectangle with lower-left corner at
(x, y) and the given width and height (in points). The image is registered
with the document and referenced from the page resources.

methods.name: DrawLine
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawLine(x1, y1, x2, y2 float64)
methods.doc:
DrawLine strokes a straight line between two points using the current stroke
color and line width.

methods.name: DrawLines
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawLines(x, y, leading float64, lines []string)
methods.doc:
DrawLines draws multiple lines of text starting with the first baseline at
(x, y) and advancing downward by leading points per line.

methods.name: DrawParagraph
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawParagraph(x, y, maxWidth, leading float64, text string) float64
methods.doc:
DrawParagraph lays out text into lines no wider than maxWidth points, breaking
on spaces, and draws it starting at (x, y) advancing downward by leading
points per line. It returns the y coordinate of the baseline following the
last drawn line.

methods.name: DrawStrikethrough
methods.doc:
DrawStrikethrough strokes a horizontal line through the middle of a line of
text of the given width starting at baseline (x, y), using the current stroke
color. The line is positioned near the font's x-height midpoint.

methods.name: DrawText
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawText(x, y float64, text string)
methods.doc:
DrawText draws a single line of text with its baseline origin at (x, y) using
the font and size set by SetFont and the current fill color. It is a no-op if
SetFont has not been called.

methods.name: DrawTextAligned
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawTextAligned(x, y, width float64, text string, align TextAlign)
methods.doc:
DrawTextAligned draws a single line of text within a box of the given width
whose left edge is at x, positioning the baseline at y and aligning the line
horizontally per align. AlignJustify behaves like AlignLeft for a single line.
It is a no-op if no font has been set.

methods.name: DrawTextBox
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawTextBox(x, y, width, leading float64, text string, opts TextOptions) float64
methods.doc:
DrawTextBox lays text out into a box whose left edge is at x and top baseline
is at y, wrapping to width points and advancing downward by leading points per
line. If leading is 0 the font's natural line height plus opts.LineGap is
used. Lines are aligned per opts.Align, and optionally underlined or struck
through. It returns the y coordinate of the baseline just past the last drawn
line. It is a no-op returning y unchanged if no font has been set.

methods.name: DrawTextCentered
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawTextCentered(cx, y float64, text string)
methods.doc:
DrawTextCentered draws a single line of text centered horizontally on cx, with
its baseline at y. It is a no-op if no font has been set.

methods.name: DrawTextRight
methods.signature: func (p *Page) DrawTextRight(x, y float64, text string)
methods.doc:
DrawTextRight draws a single line of text so that its right edge ends at x,
with the baseline at y. It is a no-op if no font has been set.

methods.name: DrawUnderline
methods.doc:
DrawUnderline strokes a horizontal underline of the given width starting at
(x, y), using the current stroke color. The stroke width scales with the
current font size. It draws relative to a baseline at y, placing the line
slightly below it.

methods.name: Ellipse
methods.signature: func (p *Page) Ellipse(cx, cy, rx, ry float64)
methods.doc:
Ellipse adds an ellipse subpath centered at (cx, cy) with horizontal radius rx
and vertical radius ry, approximated by four Bézier curves.

methods.name: Fill
methods.doc: Fill fills the current path using the nonzero winding rule ("f").

methods.name: FillEvenOdd
methods.doc: FillEvenOdd fills the current path using the even-odd rule ("f*").

methods.name: FillStroke
methods.doc: FillStroke fills and then strokes the current path ("B").

methods.name: LineTo
methods.doc: LineTo appends a straight line segment from the current point to (x, y).

methods.name: MoveTo
methods.doc: MoveTo begins a new subpath at (x, y).

methods.name: Polygon
methods.doc:
Polygon adds a closed path through the given points to the current path. pts
is a flat list of alternating x, y coordinates, so len(pts) must be even and
describe at least three points; otherwise Polygon is a no-op. Paint it with
Fill, Stroke or FillStroke.

methods.name: Polyline
methods.doc:
Polyline adds a connected open path through the given points to the current
path. pts is a flat list of alternating x, y coordinates, so len(pts) must be
even and describe at least two points; otherwise Polyline is a no-op. Paint it
with Stroke.

methods.name: Rect
methods.signature: func (p *Page) Rect(x, y, w, h float64)
methods.doc: Rect adds a rectangle subpath with its lower-left corner at (x, y).

methods.name: RegularPolygon
methods.signature: func (p *Page) RegularPolygon(cx, cy, r float64, sides int)
methods.doc:
RegularPolygon adds a closed regular polygon with the given number of sides,
centered at (cx, cy) with circumradius r, to the current path. The first
vertex is placed at the top (straight up from the center). It is a no-op for
fewer than 3 sides. Paint it with Fill, Stroke or FillStroke.

methods.name: Restore
methods.doc: Restore pops the graphics state saved by the matching Save ("Q").

methods.name: RoundedRect
methods.signature: func (p *Page) RoundedRect(x, y, w, h, r float64)
methods.doc:
RoundedRect adds a rectangle subpath with rounded corners of radius r to the
current path. The rectangle's lower-left corner is at (x, y). The radius is
clamped to at most half of the shorter side. Paint it afterwards with Stroke,
Fill or FillStroke.

methods.name: SVGPath
methods.doc:
SVGPath parses the SVG path "d" mini-language in data and appends the
equivalent PDF path-construction operators (moveto, lineto, curveto, close) to
the page's content stream in the current user space. After calling SVGPath,
paint the path with Fill, Stroke or FillStroke.

All SVG commands are supported: M/m, L/l, H/h, V/v, C/c, S/s, Q/q, T/t
(quadratic curves are elevated to cubic), A/a (elliptical arcs are flattened
to cubic Béziers) and Z/z. Coordinates are used verbatim; because SVG's y-axis
points down while PDF's points up, apply a flipping transform (for example
Page.Scale(1, -1) within a Save/Restore) if you need SVG's orientation.

It returns an error if the path data is malformed.

methods.name: Save
methods.doc: Save pushes a copy of the current graphics state (the PDF "q" operator).

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale concatenates a scale onto the current transformation matrix.

methods.name: SetAlpha
methods.doc:
SetAlpha sets the constant fill and stroke alpha (transparency) for subsequent
drawing by applying a fresh ExtGState. Values are clamped to [0, 1].

methods.name: SetBlendMode
methods.doc:
SetBlendMode sets the blend mode for subsequent compositing by applying a
fresh ExtGState.

methods.name: SetDash
methods.doc:
SetDash sets the line dash pattern. The pattern is a list of on/off segment
lengths (in points); an empty pattern restores a solid line. phase offsets the
start of the pattern.

methods.name: SetFillCMYK
methods.doc:
SetFillCMYK sets the non-stroking (fill) color to a DeviceCMYK color for
subsequent fills and text, emitting the "k" operator.

methods.name: SetFillColor
methods.doc: SetFillColor sets the color used for filling paths and drawing text.

methods.name: SetFillPattern
methods.doc:
SetFillPattern selects pat as the non-stroking (fill) color source. Subsequent
Fill calls paint with the pattern until another fill color or pattern is set.

methods.name: SetFont
methods.doc:
SetFont selects the font and size (in points) for subsequent text drawing.
The font is registered with the document and included in the page resources.

methods.name: SetLineCap
methods.doc:
SetLineCap sets the line cap style used at the ends of subsequently stroked
open subpaths.

methods.name: SetLineJoin
methods.doc:
SetLineJoin sets the line join style used at the corners of subsequently
stroked paths.

methods.name: SetLineWidth
methods.doc: SetLineWidth sets the stroke line width in points.

methods.name: SetMiterLimit
methods.doc:
SetMiterLimit sets the miter limit: the maximum ratio of miter length to line
width before a JoinMiter corner is converted to a bevel. Values below 1 are
clamped to 1.

methods.name: SetStrokeCMYK
methods.doc:
SetStrokeCMYK sets the stroking color to a DeviceCMYK color for subsequent
strokes, emitting the "K" operator.

methods.name: SetStrokeColor
methods.doc: SetStrokeColor sets the color used for stroking paths and text outlines.

methods.name: SetStrokePattern
methods.doc: SetStrokePattern selects pat as the stroking color source.

methods.name: Shade
methods.doc:
Shade paints shading s across the current clipping region using the "sh"
operator. Because "sh" fills the whole clip, first construct a path and call
Clip (inside a Save/Restore pair) to bound the gradient.

methods.name: Size
methods.doc: Size returns the page dimensions in points.

methods.name: Stroke
methods.doc: Stroke strokes the current path ("S").

methods.name: TextWidth
methods.doc:
TextWidth returns the width in points of text in the current font and size.
It returns 0 if no font has been set.

methods.name: Transform
methods.signature: func (p *Page) Transform(a, b, c, dd, e, f float64)
methods.doc:
Transform concatenates the affine matrix [a b c d e f] onto the current
transformation matrix (the "cm" operator).

methods.name: Translate
methods.doc: Translate concatenates a translation onto the current transformation matrix.

types.name: PageSize
types.doc: PageSize describes the dimensions of a page in PDF points (1/72 inch).

funcs.name: Custom
funcs.doc: Custom builds a PageSize from an explicit width and height in points.

methods.name: Landscape
methods.doc:
Landscape returns the page size rotated to landscape orientation (width and
height swapped so that width >= height).

methods.name: Portrait
methods.doc: Portrait returns the page size in portrait orientation (width <= height).

types.name: Pattern
types.doc:
Pattern is a fill source: either a shading pattern (a gradient usable to fill
arbitrary paths) built with NewShadingPattern, or a tiling pattern built with
NewTilingPattern (a small cell repeated across the fill area).

funcs.name: NewShadingPattern
funcs.doc:
NewShadingPattern wraps a gradient as a fill pattern so it can fill arbitrary
paths (via Page.SetFillPattern followed by Page.Fill).

funcs.name: NewTilingPattern
funcs.signature: func NewTilingPattern(width, height float64, draw func(*Page)) *Pattern
funcs.doc:
NewTilingPattern builds a tiling pattern whose cell is width x height points.
The draw callback receives a detached page onto which vector graphics and
colors may be drawn (text and images are not supported in a tiling cell). The
cell is repeated on a width x height grid to fill any path painted with the
pattern.

types.name: Reader
types.doc:
Reader is a deliberately minimal cross-reference reader. It parses the trailer,
the classic (uncompressed) xref table and the object offsets of a PDF produced
by this package. It is intended for validation and testing rather than as a
general-purpose PDF parser.

funcs.name: Parse
funcs.doc: Parse parses the cross-reference structure of a PDF document held in data.

methods.name: Object
methods.doc:
Object returns the raw bytes of indirect object n, from "N 0 obj" up to (but
not including) "endobj". It returns nil if n is out of range.

methods.name: ObjectCount
methods.doc: ObjectCount returns the number of indirect objects (excluding free object 0).

types.name: Shading
types.doc:
Shading is a smooth color transition (an axial/linear or radial gradient)
usable as a paint source. Create one with LinearGradient or RadialGradient,
then paint it with Page.Shade (fills the current clip region) or use it as a
fill via NewShadingPattern and Page.SetFillPattern.

funcs.name: LinearGradient
funcs.signature: func LinearGradient(x0, y0, x1, y1 float64, stops ...GradientStop) *Shading
funcs.doc:
LinearGradient builds an axial gradient running from (x0, y0) to (x1, y1) with
the given color stops (at least two, ordered by offset).

funcs.name: RadialGradient
funcs.signature: func RadialGradient(x0, y0, r0, x1, y1, r1 float64, stops ...GradientStop) *Shading
funcs.doc:
RadialGradient builds a radial gradient between two circles: one centered at
(x0, y0) with radius r0 and one at (x1, y1) with radius r1.

types.name: SizeContext
types.doc:
SizeContext supplies the document/page state that PDFKit's sizeToPoint uses to
resolve relative CSS units. It mirrors the fields the upstream method reads
off the document and current page.

types.name: TextAlign
types.signature: type TextAlign int
types.doc:
TextAlign selects how lines of text are positioned horizontally within a box
of a given width by Page.DrawTextAligned and Page.DrawTextBox.

types.name: TextOptions
types.doc:
TextOptions controls how Page.DrawTextBox lays out and decorates a block of
wrapped text.

funcs.name: NormalizeColor
funcs.doc:
NormalizeColor reproduces PDFKit's _normalizeColor exactly, returning the
component slice PDFKit would compute for a color specifier. It accepts:

  - a string beginning with '#': a 3- or 6-digit hex color, expanded and
    scaled to [0,1] as [r/255, g/255, b/255];
  - a string naming a CSS color keyword: resolved through the named-color
    table and scaled to [0,1];
  - a []float64 or []int of length 3 (RGB): each component divided by 255;
  - a []float64 or []int of length 4 (CMYK): each component divided by 100.

Exactly like the upstream routine, the numeric-slice branches perform no
range clamping, so out-of-range inputs (for example a CMYK value of 128)
produce out-of-range outputs (1.28). Unrecognised specifiers return nil.

Note that PDFKit interprets a length-4 numeric slice as CMYK scaled by 100,
which is why [255,255,255,255] normalizes to [2.55,2.55,2.55,2.55] rather than
an RGBA color.

funcs.name: SizeToPoint
funcs.signature: func SizeToPoint(size string, defaultValue float64, ctx SizeContext) (float64, bool)
funcs.doc:
SizeToPoint converts a CSS-style size specifier to PDF points, reproducing
PDFKit's sizeToPoint (lib/mixins/fonts.js). The size may be a bare number
string ("1", "12.5"), a boolean literal ("true"→1, "false"→0), or a number
with a unit suffix drawn from:

	em in px cm mm pc ex ch rem vw vh vmin vmax % pt

The units resolve as PDFKit specifies: em and % are relative to ctx.FontSize,
rem to ctx.RemSize (defaulting to 12), ex to the current font's line height,
ch to the advance of '0', and vw/vh/vmin/vmax to the page dimensions. An
unrecognised or missing unit is treated as points. It returns defaultValue for
an empty input and an ok flag reporting whether the input was understood.

funcs.name: TruncateToWidth
funcs.signature: func TruncateToWidth(f *Font, size, maxWidth float64, text, ellipsis string) string
funcs.doc:
TruncateToWidth returns text shortened so that, with ellipsis appended, it
fits within maxWidth points in font f at the given size. If the full text
already fits it is returned unchanged. Truncation happens at rune boundaries.
If even the ellipsis alone does not fit, the ellipsis is returned.

funcs.name: WrapText
funcs.signature: func WrapText(f *Font, size, maxWidth float64, text string) []string
funcs.doc:
WrapText greedily breaks text into lines no wider than maxWidth points when
set in font f at the given size, breaking only at spaces and tabs. Existing
newline characters force a break. A single word longer than maxWidth is kept
on its own line rather than split mid-word. It is the exported form of the
word-wrapping used by Page.DrawParagraph.

examples.output:
header: %PDF-1.7
pages: 1
objects: 6
ends with EOF: true
examples.doc:
Example builds a one-page document and reports structural facts about the
generated PDF bytes.

examples.name: Font (Width)
examples.doc:
ExampleFont_Width demonstrates measuring text width using the built-in AFM
metrics.
